channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Su-57E Cost Advantage Emerges as Key Factor in India’s MRFA Debate, But Stealth and AMCA Concerns Persist
item.description: The emergence of concrete pricing data from Algeria’s Su-57E deal has significantly sharpened the cost debate within India’s ongoing Multi-Role Fighter Aircraft (MRFA) competition, positioning Russia’s fifth-generation offering as a financially disruptive option. With Algeria confirmed as the first export customer in late 2025, the contract has effectively established a benchmark for both flyaway and full-package costs, allowing for more realistic comparisons with Western platforms.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s S4 SSBN Set for K-5 SLBM Upgrade with MIRV Capability, Expanding Reach to Intercontinental Deterrence
item.description: India’s latest SSBN platform, INS Aridhaman (S4), marks a decisive transition from a regional nuclear deterrent to a platform capable of supporting intercontinental strike capability, as the next-generation K-5 submarine-launched ballistic missile (SLBM) moves toward testing. While the S4 is currently operational with the K-4 missile, which has a range of approximately 3,500 kilometers, preparations are underway for “pop-up” and full-flight trials of the K-5, expected in late 2026 or early 2027.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ADA Scientists Study Airframe Life Extension for Tejas Mk1 as HAL Advances Fatigue Testing
item.description: The Tejas Mk1 was born out of ambition and necessity India’s determination to field an indigenous fighter aircraft. Now, as the fleet steadily builds flying hours, the focus is shifting from proving the platform to strengthening its longevity. Scientists at the Aeronautical Development Agency are studying ways to increase the airframe life of the Tejas Mk1, ensuring it remains operational for decades to come.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAF Explores Leasing Ultra Heavy Lift Helicopters, Signals Urgent Capability Gap in Strategic Airlift
item.description: The Ministry of Defence has issued a Request for Information (RFI) for the damp leasing of Ultra Heavy Lift Helicopters (UHLHs) for the Indian Air Force, marking a significant move to address a critical gap in heavy-lift rotary-wing capability. The proposal outlines an initial two-year lease period with an option to purchase, indicating both urgency and a potential long-term acquisition pathway.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Beyond 50km: Inside India’s Plan for Deep-Strike 800-Meter Heavyweight Torpedoes
item.description: India is advancing its underwater strike capabilities as the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) works on a new generation of long-range heavyweight torpedoes designed for deep-water combat.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Gridbots Develops Indigenous ‘Sentinel’ Anti-Drone System With Autonomous Hard-Kill Capability
item.description: India’s private defence technology sector continues to expand its counter-drone capabilities as Ahmedabad-based robotics and AI company Gridbots Technologies has developed an indigenous Sentinel Anti-Drone System, designed to autonomously detect, track, and neutralize hostile unmanned aerial vehicles.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Army T-90S “Bheesma” Tanks Spotted with Reinforced Cope Cage, Signalling Evolving Battlefield Protection Doctrine
item.description: Recent images circulating from field deployments show Indian Army T-90S “Bheesma” main battle tanks fitted with a newly reinforced cope cage structure, indicating a clear shift in survivability priorities shaped by lessons from contemporary conflicts. An examination of the image suggests that the Army is moving beyond ad-hoc field modifications toward a more deliberate and structurally integrated approach to top-attack and all-aspect protection.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ideaForge inks pact with Japan’s DMP to develop next-gen AI-powered drones
item.description: Drone tech firm ideaForge Technology Ltd on Tuesday announced inking an initial pact with Japanese AI-tech company Digital Media Professionals Inc. (DMP) to develop next-generation Di1-powered AI drones.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: At SCO meet, Rajnath Singh cites Op Sindoor to warn against state-sponsored terrorism
item.description: Operation Sindoor demonstrated India’s firm resolve that “terror epicentres” are no longer immune to “justifiable punishment”, Defence Minister Rajnath Singh said on Tuesday, in remarks widely seen as a warning to Pakistan over cross-border terrorism.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: According to data from SIPRI, India has become the fifth-largest military spender in 2025
item.description: India emerged as the fifth-largest military spender in 2025, according to the latest annual report from the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI). India’s expenditure rose to $92.1 billion, representing an 8.9% increase from 2024.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India at UNSC urges early restoration of Hormuz maritime passage route
item.description: Amid the West Asia crisis, India has urged restoration of safe and unimpeded maritime passage through the Strait of Hormuz at the earliest while expressing concern about safety of its seafarers, stressing that any “purported closure” of vital waterways has direct consequences for the global economy.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pakistan successfully conducts training launch of Fateh-II missile system that India shot downed last year
item.description: Pakistan has successfully conducted a training launch of the indigenously developed Fateh-II missile system, the army said on Tuesday. The launch of the system equipped with advanced avionics and state-of-the-art navigational aids was carried out by the Army Rocket Force, according to a statement by the army.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: 4 linked to terror module arrested
item.description: A pro-Khalistan terror module carried out a late-night blast at a freight corridor railway track near the Shambhu area in Patiala, in which one of its members was killed while attempting to detonate the explosives, police said on Tuesday, adding that four members of the module have been arrested.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Strongly Condemns Mali Terror Attacks, Condoles Death Of Defence Minister
item.description: India on Tuesday expressed condolences on the demise of General Sadio Camara, Minister of Defence and Veteran Affairs of Mali, while strongly condemning the ongoing terrorist attacks in the African nation targeting multiple locations.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: US Bill Targets Threats To Hindu Temples
item.description: In the wake of the spate of attacks on places of worship of minorities, a bipartisan bill in the US Congress seeks to protect Hindu temples and other places of worship from harassment, as lawmakers cite rising threats to faith communities.
item.dc:creator: admin
